title: Diffusion Models Recap
concept: Diffusion models learn to reverse a gradual noising process, breaking generation into many small, learnable denoising steps (Units 315-329).

exercises.prompt: What is the core concept behind how diffusion models generate images?
exercises.explain: Diffusion models learn to generate data by reversing a gradual noising process, doing it step-by-step.

cards.title: Art from Chaos
cards.body: Have you ever wondered how AI creates stunning artwork from pure randomness? It doesn't paint all at once. Instead, it plays a game of reverse-connect-the-dots with millions of tiny, noisy pixels!

cards.title: What is a Diffusion Model?
cards.body: Diffusion models are generative AIs that create data (like images) by reversing a process that destroys information. They start with random noise and, step by step, clean it up until a sharp, detailed image emerges.

cards.title: The Master Sculptor
cards.body: Think of a sculptor working in reverse. Instead of starting with a block of stone and carving a statue, imagine starting with a beautiful clay sculpture, slowly smashing it into a pile of dust, and then learning how to perfectly sweep that dust back up into the original sculpture.

cards.title: From Static to Cat
cards.body: When you ask an AI to generate 'a cat sitting on a couch', the model starts with a screen full of static (like an old TV with no signal). Over 50 to 100 small steps, it gently nudges the static, turning random dots into whiskers, paws, and eventually a cat.

cards.title: Not a Collage Maker
cards.body: Many people believe diffusion models store a library of real images and stitch them together. In reality, they only learn the *rules* of how to clean up noise. They generate entirely new images that have never existed before!

cards.title: Two Directions of Diffusion
cards.body: See how the diffusion process changes direction depending on whether we are training the model or using it to generate something brand new.

left.text: Starts with a clear image and slowly adds noise until it is completely ruined.

right.text: Starts with random noise and slowly removes it to create a brand new image.

exercises.prompt: What is the difference between the forward and reverse processes in diffusion models?

options.text: The forward process adds noise, and the reverse process removes it.
The forward process sharpens the image, and the reverse process blurs it.
Both processes add noise to make the image look abstract.
The forward process is for training, and the reverse process is never used.

exercises.explain: The forward process gradually adds noise to an image until it becomes unrecognizable, while the reverse process removes that noise to generate new images.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Breaking the image generation into many small denoising steps makes the learning process easier for the AI.
exercises.explain: By breaking the generation into many small, simple steps, the model only has to make tiny corrections at each step, which is much easier to learn.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how diffusion models generate images.
exercises.sentence: To generate a new image, a diffusion model gradually removes ______ step by step.
exercises.explain: Diffusion models work by starting with pure noise and repeatedly removing small amounts of it to reveal a clear image.

exercises.prompt: How does a text prompt influence the denoising process?

options.text: AI Model B: It colors the final image after it is fully generated.
AI Model A: It guides the denoising steps to shape the random noise into the requested prompt.
AI Model C: It edits the text prompt to make it match the random noise.

exercises.explain: Text prompts act as a guide, telling the model which direction to clean the noise so it forms the specific image you asked for.

exercises.prompt: How does a diffusion model actually generate a clean image during the reverse process?

options.text: It instantly generates a clean image from text in a single step
It predicts and removes a tiny amount of noise at each step
It compares two images and decides which one looks more realistic
It increases the noise level until the image is completely blank

exercises.explain: Diffusion models are trained to estimate and subtract the noise added at each step, progressively transforming pure noise back into a coherent image.
